stable_papers.title: The disruption index is biased by citation inflation

sample.fidelity_rationale: N and window explicit; R002 reproduced exactly

indicator.formula: CDp = (Ni - Nj)/(Ni + Nj + Nk); |CDp,5| with citation-inflation decomposition

result_table.fidelity_rationale: R002 achieved D3=8/8 exact numerical identity

conclusion_claims: CD systematically declines over time due to citation inflation, not true decline in innovation
claim.fidelity_rationale: Claim directly supported by negative coef

stable_papers.notes: Calibration anchor (Low floor). R002 D3=8/8. Carry-over from R098/C0.

stable_papers.title: Teams vs. disruption (Wu et al. 2019)

stable_papers.title: Papers and patents are becoming less disruptive over time

conclusion_claims: Papers and patents are becoming less disruptive over time

stable_papers.title: Is disruption decreasing, or is it accelerating?

claim.fidelity_rationale: Weighted CD close to zero since ~1970, modest increase since 2000

stable_papers.title: Hot Streaks in Artistic, Cultural, and Scientific Careers

data_source.data_source: Career histories: artists (artchive/artnet), directors (IMDb), scientists (Web of Science + APS)
data_source.fidelity_rationale: Public code + public data in lu-liu/hotstreaks repo (data.zip 85MB)

indicator.formula: Hot-streak model (random top-hit ordering) vs (b) right-trapezoid (c) isosceles-trapezoid (d) quadratic (e) tent (f) left-trapezoid; relative position P(Ñ) of top-3 hits within top-6
indicator.fidelity_rationale: Hot-streak model + 5 alternative burst functions; KS-test comparison

target_tables: Fig: P(Ñ) distributions per domain + KS p-values
result_table.fidelity_rationale: P(Ñ) distributions + KS p-values; notebooks/code regenerate

conclusion_claims: Hot streaks are a real phenomenon across artistic, cultural, and scientific careers; the hot-streak model reproduces the randomness in top-hit ordering that alternative models cannot

B2.note: hard-code KS p-values (low risk given code)
